paragraphs.source: Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18. Labor is 3x more expensive. Foundation construction costs 4x as much. Raw materials run 2x. Utilities are the only win: water costs half as much, electricity 50% cheaper. These savings are dwarfed by the labor and construction delta.

inline_elements.text: Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: [Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18](https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/tsmc-overseas-fabs-a-success). Labor is 3x more expensive. Foundation construction costs 4x as much. Raw materials run 2x. Utilities are the only win: water costs half as much, electricity 50% cheaper. These savings are dwarfed by the labor and construction delta.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18. Labor is 3x more expensive. Foundation construction costs 4x as much. Raw materials run 2x. Utilities are the only win: water costs half as much, electricity 50% cheaper. These savings are dwarfed by the labor and construction delta.

parent_inline_elements.text: Arizona wafer costs run 2.4x higher than Taiwan’s Fab 18

pro.text: 亚利桑那州的晶圆成本是台湾地区 Fab 18 的 2.4 倍。劳动力成本是其 3 倍。地基建设成本是其 4 倍。原材料成本翻倍。水电等公用事业是唯一的成本洼地：水费便宜一半，电费低 50%。然而，在劳动力与建设成本的巨大鸿沟面前，这点节约可谓杯水车薪。
pro.tokenized_text: [[[LINK_1|亚利桑那州的晶圆成本是台湾地区 Fab 18 的 2.4 倍]]]。劳动力成本是其 3 倍。地基建设成本是其 4 倍。原材料成本翻倍。水电等公用事业是唯一的成本洼地：水费便宜一半，电费低 50%。然而，在劳动力与建设成本的巨大鸿沟面前，这点节约可谓杯水车薪。

issues.description: 中文语境中，“高出 2.4 倍”通常在数学上意味着 1+2.4=3.4 倍，这会夸大原文的成本差距。结合第3段的“At 2.4x the cost”可知，这里的 2.4x 和 3x 指的是成本的绝对倍数，而非增量倍数。应统一改为“是……的 X 倍”。
译者遵照了提示中的建议添加了括注，但“（这里的 A14 指台积电的 1.4 纳米节点，而非苹果处理器）”作为句内插入语略显冗长，打断了行文的专业节奏。按照 Guideline 1，可以直接将节点名称显性化，既消除歧义又保持简洁。

paragraphs.source: Apple is already producing chips at Fab 21. TSMC lists Apple, AMD, Qualcomm, and NVIDIA as Phase 1 customers. Apple’s mobile SoCs, around 100mm² die size, are among the early production volume. Yields reportedly exceeded Taiwan by four percentage points at the same ramp stage, though the product mix skews toward smaller dies rather than Nvidia’s 800mm² GPUs. The fab reached 15K wafers per month in 2Q25 and is ramping toward 24K wspm.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple is already producing chips at Fab 21. TSMC lists Apple, AMD, Qualcomm, and NVIDIA as Phase 1 customers. Apple’s mobile SoCs, around 100mm² die size, are among the early production volume. Yields reportedly exceeded Taiwan by four percentage points at the same ramp stage, though the product mix skews toward smaller dies rather than Nvidia’s 800mm² GPUs. The fab reached 15K wafers per month in 2Q25 and is ramping toward 24K wspm.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple is already producing chips at Fab 21. TSMC lists Apple, AMD, Qualcomm, and NVIDIA as Phase 1 customers. Apple’s mobile SoCs, around 100mm² die size, are among the early production volume. Yields reportedly exceeded Taiwan by four percentage points at the same ramp stage, though the product mix skews toward smaller dies rather than Nvidia’s 800mm² GPUs. The fab reached 15K wafers per month in 2Q25 and is ramping toward 24K wspm.

pro.text: 苹果已在 Fab 21 投产芯片。台积电将苹果、AMD、高通与英伟达列为一期客户。裸片尺寸约 100 平方毫米的苹果移动 SoC 是早期量产的主力。据报道，在同等产能爬坡阶段，其良率较台湾地区高出 4 个百分点；但需注意，该厂的产品组合主要向小尺寸裸片倾斜，而非英伟达 800 平方毫米的巨型 GPU。2025 年第二季度，该晶圆厂的每月晶圆投片量已达 1.5 万片，并正向 2.4 万片的目标持续爬坡。

issues.description: 中文语境中，“高出 2.4 倍”通常在数学上意味着 1+2.4=3.4 倍，这会夸大原文的成本差距。结合第3段的“At 2.4x the cost”可知，这里的 2.4x 和 3x 指的是成本的绝对倍数，而非增量倍数。应统一改为“是……的 X 倍”。
译者遵照了提示中的建议添加了括注，但“（这里的 A14 指台积电的 1.4 纳米节点，而非苹果处理器）”作为句内插入语略显冗长，打断了行文的专业节奏。按照 Guideline 1，可以直接将节点名称显性化，既消除歧义又保持简洁。

paragraphs.source: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The node lag matters more than cost. Taiwan’s “N-2 principle” requires overseas fabs to trail domestic production by at least two generations. If TSMC is developing 1.2nm or 1.4nm in Taiwan, only 1.6nm can go abroad. Process technologies at 14nm and below are classified as critical technologies, covering equipment and materials. TSMC’s 10,000+ R&D engineers remain in Hsinchu. The “mother fab” for each new node must be in Taiwan. By the time Arizona receives N2, Taiwan will be running A14 or beyond. Fab 21 represents less than 15% of TSMC’s total N5/N3 capacity. Arizona hedges Taiwan risk. It does not eliminate Taiwan dependency.

pro.text: 制程代差的影响远甚于成本。台湾地区的“N-2”原则要求海外晶圆厂的技术代际必须落后本土至少两代。若台积电在台湾地区研发 1.2 纳米或 1.4 纳米，海外最多只能引入 1.6 纳米。14 纳米及以下工艺技术（涵盖设备与材料）均被列为关键技术。台积电逾万名研发工程师仍留守新竹。每个新制程节点的母厂必须设在台湾地区。当亚利桑那州工厂终于等来 N2 时，台湾地区早已量产台积电 A14（1.4纳米）甚至更先进制程。Fab 21 在台积电 N5/N3 总产能中的占比不足 15%。亚利桑那州工厂仅能对冲台湾地区的风险，无法消除对台湾地区的依赖。

paragraphs.source: Apple can absorb higher costs as a hedge against Taiwan risk. But Apple won’t shift majority production to Arizona unless forced. At 2.4x the cost of Taiwan wafers, the economics don’t justify it absent geopolitical catastrophe.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple can absorb higher costs as a hedge against Taiwan risk. But Apple won’t shift majority production to Arizona unless forced. At 2.4x the cost of Taiwan wafers, the economics don’t justify it absent geopolitical catastrophe.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple can absorb higher costs as a hedge against Taiwan risk. But Apple won’t shift majority production to Arizona unless forced. At 2.4x the cost of Taiwan wafers, the economics don’t justify it absent geopolitical catastrophe.

pro.text: 苹果有能力消化溢价，借此对冲台湾地区的地缘风险。但除非迫不得已，苹果绝不会将主力产能迁至亚利桑那州。面对高达台湾地区 2.4 倍的晶圆成本，若非爆发地缘政治灾难，这种转移在经济账上根本站不住脚。

paragraphs.source: Currently 5% of leading-edge production is from Arizona. If this rises to 10-15% by 2028, it signals Apple is genuinely worried about Taiwan. If it stays at 5%, Arizona is pure insurance policy.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Currently 5% of leading-edge production is from Arizona. If this rises to 10-15% by 2028, it signals Apple is genuinely worried about Taiwan. If it stays at 5%, Arizona is pure insurance policy.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Currently 5% of leading-edge production is from Arizona. If this rises to 10-15% by 2028, it signals Apple is genuinely worried about Taiwan. If it stays at 5%, Arizona is pure insurance policy.

pro.text: 目前，仅有 5% 的先进制程产能来自亚利桑那州。若到 2028 年该比例攀升至 10% 至 15%，则明确释放出信号：苹果对台湾地区局势深感忧虑。若比例停滞在 5%，那么亚利桑那州工厂充其量只是一份纯粹的保单。
